full_text: Apple's self-driving car program never really got off the ground , but it may have been what made the company's chips the powerful AI performers they are. Early in the development of the self-driving platform, Apple realized that it would need powerful on-device AI processing . While the car processor was never finished , as Mark Gurman details in his latest Power On newsletter, it did lead to the development of the Neural Engine, the backbone of Apple's on-device AI processing. The Neural Engine made its debut with the iPhone X and the A11 Bionic . In those early days, it was primarily used for computer vision, powering FaceID, Animoji, and a … Read the full story at The Verge.

item.title: Apple’s failed self-driving car program left a legacy of powerful AI chips
